growing_areas: All Agro-Climatic Zones Of Sri Lanka

pests.name: Leaf Eating Caterpillar
pests.impact: Young larvae are light green, later turn dark green. When damage is severe, they eat all parts except the veins.
pests.solution: If damage is severe, apply recommended insecticide.

pests.name: Leaf Miner
pests.impact: Forms irregular tunnel-like structures on leaves. These are gray/white, and black fecal matter of the larva can be seen inside as stripes.

pests.name: Sawfly
pests.impact: Larval stage is harmful. Fully grown larvae are 4-6 cm long, black or brown. Hides in soil during the day and causes damage at night. Eats young plants at soil level. Eats leaves of grown plants irregularly.
pests.solution: Till and rake soil. Hand-pick larvae hidden in soil. Tilling exposes larvae to birds. Apply suitable insecticide to plant and soil on planting day. Add more organic fertilizer to soil. Practice field sanitation.

diseases.name: Clubroot Disease
diseases.impact: Causes severe damage when cultivated in low pH (acidic) soils. Roots of plants swell and become tuber-like.
diseases.solution: Pathogen persists in soil for a long time and cannot be destroyed by strong chemical spores. Difficult to control. Test soil before cultivation and raise pH with lime if necessary. Uproot and burn infected plants. Reduce disease by practicing crop rotation with non-Brassicaceae crops for about four years.
